title: classify qwen-image NSFW rejections as 422, not 500
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
qwen-image / qwen-image-plus showed a high failure rate on the model monitor. All the 5xx are the same Replicate safety-filter rejection:

`Qwen Image Edit generation failed: All generated images contained NSFW content...`

These are content-policy rejections, not backend failures, but they leaked through the moderation funnel as raw 500s because `MODERATION_PATTERNS` had no `nsfw` entry — so they polluted `errors_5xx` and tripped false "degraded" alarms.

- Adds `nsfw` to `MODERATION_PATTERNS` so these surface as `422 content_policy_violation` (same path as DashScope/Replicate/Vertex/Azure rejections)
- Adds the real Qwen message to the moderation test corpus

Impact (prod, last 24h): 34 NSFW-worded 5xx, all from qwen-image/qwen-image-plus, one identical message, none matched by existing patterns — and none of the other ~900 5xx mention nsfw, so no genuine backend failure is reclassified. These flip 500 → 422, drop out of `emitServerError`/`errors_5xx`, and users get a clear "adjust your input" message.

Tests: `contentModeration.test.ts` (34) pass.

gist.headline: Better boundaries for Qwen
gist.blurb: We fixed a bug where Qwen-image safety filter rejections were showing up as generic server errors. Now you'll get a proper 422 status code so you know exactly why your prompt was blocked.
gist.summary: Added 'nsfw' to the moderation patterns list so Qwen-image content policy rejections return as 422 errors instead of 500s.
gist.impact: Users will now receive a clear 'content policy violation' message instead of a generic server error when prompts are rejected by the safety filter.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee acting as a friendly traffic cop holding a '422' sign, while the Nomnom creature eats a red '500' error log.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny crossing guard hat, holding up a small '422' sign in front of a glowing picture frame. The round tan nomnom creature happily eats a red '500' error log nearby. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.

image.prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny crossing guard hat, holding up a small '422' sign in front of a glowing picture frame. The round tan nomnom creature happily eats a red '500' error log nearby. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
